auth.passwordWarning: Your password is crucial. It allows you to decrypt all your data. Keep it safe.
auth.passwordManagerReco: We highly recommend using a password manager:
auth.e2eDesc: Your files are encrypted before they even leave your device.
auth.zeroKnowledgeDesc: We only know your email address, username and avatar. No ads, no cookies, no tracking.
auth.secureSharingDesc: Exchange your files in encrypted P2P, without intermediate server, with access control and expiration.
auth.p2pTagline: Send files directly, with no intermediate server.
auth.p2pFeature1Desc: Files travel directly from your browser to the recipient's. No intermediate storage.
auth.p2pFeature2Desc: The file is encrypted before sending. Neither we nor anyone else can read its content.
auth.p2pFeature3Desc: You can only transfer to approved contacts. No public links, no accidental leaks.

deleteAccount.point1: Permanent deletion of all your files and folders

home.subtitle: Kagibi encrypts your files on your device before any upload. Neither us nor anyone else can access them — mathematically. Hosted in France, GDPR-compliant, immune to the Cloud Act.
home.step1Desc: Your files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM directly in your browser. The key never leaves your device.
home.step2Desc: Only the encrypted stream reaches our servers in France. Even with full infrastructure access, no one can decrypt your files.
home.step3Desc: Only you can decrypt and read your data. Share securely via encrypted links or direct P2P transfer.
home.featSubtitle: A complete platform built on solid cryptographic foundations.
home.feat1Desc: Client-side AES-256-GCM encryption. Your keys never leave your device. Our servers only see encrypted noise — impossible otherwise.
home.feat2Desc: Send large files directly, browser to browser. No intermediate storage, maximum speed, no trace on our servers.
home.feat3Desc: 100% OVH France infrastructure (Gravelines). Outside US jurisdiction. Strict GDPR compliance. Your data never leaves Europe.
home.feat4Desc: Source code fully published under the AGPLv3 license. Audit yourself what Kagibi does with your data. Trust should not be blind.
home.ossPara1: Kagibi is published under the GNU AGPLv3 license — the strongest copyleft license. Any modification deployed in production must be shared publicly. No hidden proprietary version, no possible backdoor.
home.ossPara2: The code is freely auditable, forkable and improvable by everyone. Contribute to security, report bugs, suggest features — every Pull Request is welcome.
home.ctaSubtitle: Join Kagibi and store your files without ever compromising your privacy.

transfer.heroSubtitle: Send up to 10 GB directly, no upload. Maximum speed, zero trace.
transfer.step1Desc: Choose the files to send (10 GB max)
transfer.step2Desc: A secure link is generated instantly
transfer.step3Desc: Direct P2P connection via TURN server
transfer.feat1Desc: No server upload, direct P2P transfer at full bandwidth
transfer.feat2Desc: Your files are encrypted end-to-end during the transfer
transfer.feat3Desc: No file is stored on our servers, absolute peace of mind
transfer.feat4Desc: Service available at all times, no usage limits
transfer.ctaSubtitle: Sign in to start using instant P2P transfer
transfer.ctaNote: Free and unlimited for all users
transfer.tech1Desc: Peer-to-peer communication protocol standardized by the W3C. Enables a direct connection between browsers without intermediary.
transfer.tech2Desc: Relay infrastructure for connections that cannot be made directly. Guarantees a successful transfer even behind a strict NAT.
transfer.tech3Desc: Client-side encryption before transfer. Keys are exchanged via secure signaling, never stored.

compare.title: Kagibi vs. other solutions
compare.subtitle: Not all cloud storage solutions are equal. Here is why client-side encryption changes everything.
compare.disclaimer: Information collected publicly from official websites and vendor documentation. Last updated: April 2026.
compare.ctaTitle: The solution that mathematically cannot read your data
compare.ctaSubtitle: Client-side encryption is not an option — it is the foundation.
compare.r1tip: Your files are encrypted on your device before sending — the server only sees noise.
compare.r2tip: Direct browser-to-browser transfer, no intermediate storage.
compare.r5tip: Servers outside US jurisdiction cannot be compelled to hand over data.
compare.r8tip: Deploy your own instance on your infrastructure.
compare.r9tip: Prohibits proprietary forks — any modification must remain public.
compare.techProton5: Part of the source code is closed
compare.techNextcloud5: Experimental E2EE module, not enabled by default
compare.techGoogle1: Encryption in transit and at rest
compare.techGoogle2: Google holds and manages all keys
compare.techGoogle5: Content used to train AI models

security.heroSub: This is not a marketing promise — it is a mathematical constraint. Here is, concretely, what happens on your device and what the server never sees.
security.sec1Title: The server stores data it cannot read
security.sec1Sub: Every time you encrypt a file, all the cryptography happens in your browser, never on the server. The server only receives encrypted bytes. Without your password — which it does not know — it cannot do anything with them.
security.cmpBadNote: The server encrypts itself → it holds the key → it can read everything, hand it to authorities, get hacked.
security.cmpGoodNote: The server only receives encrypted data. Without your password, it is mathematically incapable of decrypting.
security.sec2Sub: All your data is protected by a chain of nested keys. Each key encrypts another — only your password can unlock everything.
security.sec3Title: What happens when you create an account
security.sec3Sub: All the cryptography is performed in your browser before a single byte is sent.
security.regStep1Desc: Your password is passed to Argon2id in a Web Worker. The result (KEK) never leaves the browser.
security.regStep2Desc: A random AES-256 key is generated via CSPRNG. It will be stored in the Service Worker with extractable: false.
security.regStep3Desc: The Master Key is encrypted (wrapped) with the KEK via AES-GCM. This ciphertext is sent to the server.
security.regStep4Desc: A 4096-bit RSA-OAEP key pair is generated. The public key goes to the server. The private key is encrypted by the Master Key.
security.regStep5Desc: 32 random bytes. A SHA-256 hash goes to the server for verification. The Master Key is also wrapped with this code.
security.regStep6Title: What is sent to the server
security.regStep6Desc: Only encrypted or public data. Never your password, the KEK, or the Master Key in plaintext.
security.sec4Sub: Files are split into 10 MB blocks, each encrypted independently with a unique nonce (deterministic by chunk number). Never any plaintext on the network.
security.uploadDone: ✓ {n} chunks encrypted and uploaded — no plaintext byte was transmitted
security.sec5Title: Sharing a file without the server seeing the key
security.sec5Sub: When Alice shares a file with Bob, the file key is encrypted with Bob's RSA public key. The server cannot decrypt it — only Bob, with his private key, can.
security.shareStep1Action: Retrieves her file key from memory
security.shareStep3Action: Encrypts the file key with Bob's public key
security.shareStep4Data: Cannot decrypt it (no Bob's private key)
security.shareStep5Action: Decrypts the file key with his RSA private key
security.shareStep5Data: RSA-OAEP.decrypt(encrypted_key, bob_private_key) → fileKey → decrypts the file
security.svServerNote: The server cannot decrypt anything — it holds neither Bob's private key nor Alice's Master Key.
security.sec6Title: The Master Key is never exposed to JavaScript
security.sec6Sub: Even if a malicious extension or an XSS vulnerability injects JavaScript into the page, it cannot steal your Master Key. Here is why.
security.swWorkerResponse:
✓ Uses the key to encrypt/decrypt
✗ NEVER exports it
security.swProp1Desc: The WebCrypto API forbids key export. crypto.subtle.exportKey() returns an error.
security.swProp2Desc: 30 minutes of inactivity → Master Key erased from the Service Worker → automatic logout.
security.swProp3Desc: A MutationObserver watches for scripts injected into the DOM. onerror handlers are blocked.
security.sec7Title: What the server sees — and does not see
security.sees7: File metadata (name encrypted if enabled, size, MIME)
security.never4: 🚫 The RSA private key in plaintext
security.ctaSubtitle: The full source code is available under the AGPLv3 license. Everything can be verified, audited, forked.
security.khPasswordTooltipBody: Passed to Argon2id in a separate Web Worker, it is never serialized, never sent to the server. The produced hash is the KEK.
security.khKekTooltipBody: Derived from your password via Argon2id (64 MB RAM, 4 passes). It is used solely to encrypt/decrypt the Master Key. It is never stored.
security.khMasterTooltipBody1: AES-256 key randomly generated at registration. Stored encrypted (by the KEK) on the server. In session in the Service Worker with
security.khRsaTooltipBody: Generated at registration, encrypted by the Master Key before being sent to the server. Used for sharing: the sender encrypts a file key with your public key.
security.khFolderTooltipBody: Each folder has its own AES-256 key, encrypted by the Master Key. Sharing a folder = sharing this key encrypted with the recipient's RSA public key.
security.khFileTooltipBody: Each file has its own AES-256 key, encrypted by the folder key. Enables key rotation and granular per-file revocation.
security.rfEncMkSub: Stored on the server · unusable without KEK
